canonical_definition: Based on the provided data, the Wizard of Wikipedia (WoW) dataset is characterized as a benchmark for evaluating knowledge-grounded dialogue. The single available definition specifies a conversational setting where one participant is given access to Wikipedia articles. The source paper, 'RAG-DDR: Optimizing Retrieval-Augmented Generation Using Differentiable Data Rewards', explicitly states that WoW is used for 'evaluation' in its work. This positions the dataset as an instrument for assessing model performance on dialogue tasks that require external knowledge.
papers: A Theory for Token-Level Harmonization in Retrieval-Augmented Generation
Long-Context LLMs Meet RAG: Overcoming Challenges for Long Inputs in RAG
RAG-DDR: Optimizing Retrieval-Augmented Generation Using Differentiable Data Rewards

definitions.text: The Wizard of Wikipedia (WoW) dataset, a benchmark for knowledge-grounded dialogue where one conversationalist has access to Wikipedia articles.
definitions.paper: RAG-DDR: Optimizing Retrieval-Augmented Generation Using Differentiable Data Rewards

characterization: Based on the provided data, the Wizard of Wikipedia (WoW) dataset is characterized as a benchmark for evaluating knowledge-grounded dialogue. The single available definition specifies a conversational setting where one participant is given access to Wikipedia articles. The source paper, 'RAG-DDR: Optimizing Retrieval-Augmented Generation Using Differentiable Data Rewards', explicitly states that WoW is used for 'evaluation' in its work. This positions the dataset as an instrument for assessing model performance on dialogue tasks that require external knowledge.
